seo.title: AI Agent Observability in Pydantic AI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent observability in Pydantic AI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Pydantic AI agents, observability challenges arise from strict type validation clashing with LLM unpredictability, nested tool execution losing context, and streaming outputs failing partial validation. These issues manifest as silent rejections of valid-but-unexpected responses, masked tool failures, and broken dependency chains in production agents. Vex integration as a result_validator provides comprehensive tracing of these opaque failures.

howItManifests.scenario: Result validator rejecting valid but unexpected model outputs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent returns None despite successful API call, pydantic.Field validation errors logged but not surfaced
howItManifests.impact: Downstream systems receive empty responses, triggering false alerts and SLA violations

howItManifests.scenario: Tool retry logic masking persistent underlying errors
howItManifests.symptom: MaxRetriesError after 3 attempts but original tool failure (API timeout, auth error) not visible
howItManifests.impact: Operators can't distinguish transient vs permanent failures, leading to incorrect retry policies

howItManifests.scenario: Dependency injection context not available in nested tool calls
howItManifests.symptom: Inner tools fail with 'context not found' despite outer agent having valid DI container
howItManifests.impact: Multi-step workflows silently fail mid-execution, corrupting agent state

howItManifests.scenario: Streaming structured responses failing mid-generation validation
howItManifests.symptom: Partial stream validates but final chunk triggers ValidationError, entire response discarded
howItManifests.impact: Real-time UIs show loading forever, users abandon incomplete responses

detection.name: Trace Result Validator Rejections
detection.description: Capture pydantic validation failures that occur in agent result validators

detection.name: Monitor Tool Retry Patterns
detection.description: Log retry attempts and underlying error types to identify masked failures
detection.code:
from pydantic_ai import AgentConfig
import logging

# Detect retry masking
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
config = AgentConfig(
    max_retries=3,
    retry_on=[TimeoutError, APIError],
    retry_logger=lambda attempt, error: logging.error(f"RETRY {attempt}: {error}")
)
agent = Agent(config=config)
result = agent.run('call unreliable API')

detection.name: Capture Nested Context Loss
detection.description: Trace dependency injection failures in nested tool execution

fixes.name: Graceful Result Validator with Vex
fixes.description: Replace strict validation with Vex-powered observability that captures and classifies failures
fixes.code:
from vex_sdk import VexResultValidator
from pydantic_ai import Agent

# Fix: Vex result validator
vex_validator = VexResultValidator(
    project_id='your-project',
    capture_validation_errors=True,
    classify_rejections=True
)

agent = Agent(result_validator=vex_validator)
result = agent.run('complex structured output')

# Now failures are traced with root cause
print(result.vex_trace.validation_errors)

fixes.name: Transparent Retry Observability
fixes.description: Instrument retries with structured logging and failure classification
fixes.code:
from pydantic_ai import AgentConfig
from vex_sdk import VexRetryHandler

# Fix: Observable retries
config = AgentConfig(
    retry_handler=VexRetryHandler(
        max_retries=3,
        classify_errors=True,
        capture_traces=True
    )
)
agent = Agent(config=config)
result = agent.run('unreliable tool call')

# Access retry insights
print(result.retry_summary.error_patterns)

fixes.name: Persistent Nested Context Propagation
fixes.description: Ensure DI context flows through nested tool calls with tracing
fixes.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, Context
from vex_sdk import VexContextPropagator

# Fix: Context propagation
propagator = VexContextPropagator(
    persist_nested=True,
    trace_context_flow=True
)

agent = Agent(
    context_propagator=propagator,
    tools=[nested_tool1, nested_tool2]
)
result = agent.run('multi-step workflow')

# Verify context integrity
print(result.context_trace.flow_complete)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Observability Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as Pydantic AI result_validators and retry_handlers, automatically capturing validation failures, retry patterns, context loss, and streaming errors. It classifies issues by severity and suggests remediations based on production patterns observed across similar agents. The platform provides instant dashboards showing MTTR by failure type and automated alerting for regression detection.

Vex's agent-specific instrumentation understands Pydantic AI's execution model, tracing from initial prompt through nested tool execution to final structured output. It captures the exact validation schemas that failed, LLM token usage patterns, and dependency injection state at each layer. This eliminates manual debugging of opaque agent failures.

Remediation workflows auto-generate fixed agent configs, test them against failure reproducers, and deploy with canary validation. Vex reduces agent MTTR from hours to minutes by pattern-matching against 10,000+ resolved incidents.
vexAutomation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from vex_sdk import VexAgentObserver

# Complete Vex integration for Pydantic AI
observer = VexAgentObserver(
    project_id='prod-agents',
    auto_instrument=True,
    detect=[
        'result_validation',
        'tool_retry_masking',
        'context_loss',
        'stream_validation'
    ],
    auto_fix=True
)

# Single line integration
agent = Agent(observer=observer)

# Production deployment
result = agent.run('production query')

# Vex auto-generates observability dashboard
print(f'Dashboard: {observer.dashboard_url()}')

# Auto-remediation available
if observer.has_critical_issues():
    fixed_config = observer.auto_remediate()

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install with `pip install pydantic-ai vex-sdk` and add one line to observe all your Pydantic AI agents in production.
